title: Venting apparatus and method
abstract: A venting apparatus for a subsea flexible pipe and a method of installing a venting apparatus is disclosed. The venting apparatus comprises a venting conduit configured for positioning through a polymer shield layer of a flexible pipe body. The venting conduit provides a venting passage therethrough and a vent cap is provided in operable connection with the venting conduit. The vent cap is for positioning on a radially outer side of the polymer shield layer. The vent cap comprises a one-way fluid valve for allowing fluid to flow in a direction from the vent passage out of the one-way fluid valve.
claims:
Claims 1. A venting apparatus for a subsea flexible pipe, comprising: a venting conduit configured for positioning through a polymer shield layer of a flexible pipe body, the venting conduit providing a venting passage therethrough; and a vent cap provided in operable connection with the venting conduit, the vent cap for positioning on a radially outer side of the polymer shield layer, the vent cap comprising a one-way fluid valve for allowing fluid to flow in a direction from the vent passage out of the one-way fluid valve. 2. A venting apparatus as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the vent cap comprises a seat portion for seating against a radially outer surface of the polymer shield layer, and a cover portion extending outwards from the seat portion, the seat portion having a first side for seating against the polymer shield layer and a second side. 3. A venting apparatus as claimed in claim 2 wherein the cover portion extends over an inner area of the second side of the seat portion, and an outer area of the second side of the seat portion is configured for providing a fastening for attaching the venting apparatus to a flexible pipe body. 4. A venting apparatus as claimed in claim 2 or 3 wherein the first side of the seat portion comprises at least one protrusion for engaging and sealing against the polymer shield layer. 5. A venting apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim, the vent cap comprising a permeable material to provide a continuous venting pathway for annulus gases to discharge from the flexible pipe. 6. A venting apparatus as claimed in claim 5 wherein the permeable material comprises nano-foamed ceramic materials. 7. A venting apparatus as claimed in claims 5 or 6 wherein the venting pathway is configured to utilise a diffusion gradient to allow annulus gases to discharge from the flexible pipe annulus. 8. A venting apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the vent cap comprises a hydrophobic material for preventing water from entering the polymer shield layer. 9. A venting apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the vent cap further comprises a coating for preventing marine growth. 10. A venting apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim wherein the venting conduit comprises a tapping-edge for attachment to the flexible pipe body. 1 1. A venting apparatus as claimed in claim 10 wherein the tapping-edge is configured to allow attachment to the flexible pipe body retrospectively. 12. A flexible pipe for transporting fluids from a subsea location comprising a flexible pipe body having a venting apparatus as claimed in any preceding claim. 13. A flexible pipe as claimed in claim 12 wherein the venting cap is located on an outer surface of an outer polymer shield layer of a flexible pipe body. 14. A method of installing a venting apparatus to a flexible pipe body, wherein the method comprises: positioning a venting apparatus through the polymer shield layer of a flexible pipe body to provide a venting passage therethrough, wherein the venting apparatus comprises a venting conduit extending through the polymer shield layer, and a vent cap provided in operable connection with the venting conduit, the vent cap positioned on a radially outer side of the polymer shield layer, and wherein the vent 

 cap comprises a one-way fluid valve for allowing fluid to flow in a direction from the vent passage out of the one-way fluid valve. 15. A method as claimed in claim 14, further comprising determining a thickness of the polymer shield layer prior to positioning the venting conduit through the polymer shield layer. 16. A method as claimed in claim 15 further comprising forming a pilot hole through the polymeric shield layer after determining the thickness and prior to positioning the venting conduit. 17. A method as claimed in claim 16 wherein the pilot hole is formed by a tapping-edge of the vent conduit. 18. A method as claimed in any of claims 14 to 17 wherein the polymeric shield layer is an outer sheath. 19. A method as claimed in claims 14 to 18, wherein the venting apparatus is fitted retrospectively. 20. A method as claimed in claims 14 to 19, wherein the vent cap comprises a seat portion for seating against a radially outer surface of the polymer shield layer, and a cover portion extending outwards from the seat portion. 21. A method as claimed in claim 20 further comprising strapping the seat portion to the polymer shield layer. 22. A venting apparatus as claimed in claim 20 or 21 wherein the seat portion comprises at least one protrusion for engaging and sealing against the polymer shield layer. 23. A method as claimed in any of claims 20 to 22 wherein the seat portion and cover portion form an enclosed space in fluid communication with the venting passage, and wherein the enclosed space comprises a permeable material to provide a continuous venting pathway for annulus gases to discharge from the flexible pipe. 24. A method as claimed in any of claims 14 to 23, wherein the vent cap comprises a hydrophobic material for preventing water from entering the polymer shield layer. 25. A method as claimed in any of claims 14 to 24 further comprising coating the vent cap with a layer of material for preventing marine growth.
